Data Processing and MNPI Handling Addendum (KSA PDPL) between Sahari Ports & Logistics JSC (CR 1010999999) and Najd Data Services LLC (CR 1010888888).
Purpose: processing of shareholder registry, insider lists, board/employee records, and issuer disclosure workflows for compliance with the PDPL and CMA rules.
Personal data processed may include National ID/Iqama numbers, names, contact details, job titles, and sign-in logs; no processing of unrelated categories (e.g., biometric templates) without written instruction.
Lawful basis and transparency: Sahari shall provide a privacy notice to data subjects, and Najd Data Services will route DSARs to Ms. Huda Al‑Sahli, Company Secretary, at cosec@sahari.example.sa or +966-11-000-0000.
Cross-border transfers: no transfer outside the Kingdom unless aligned with SDAIA guidance, with registration of the transfer and safeguards; the current test export to EU‑West is pending SDAIA reference S‑XFER‑0077 and remains disabled until approval.
MNPI handling: Processor acknowledges exposure to non‑public information material to Sahari’s securities, as defined by CMA Market Conduct Regulations, and agrees that information is only “public” once announced on the Saudi Exchange (Tadawul) portal or via other CMA‑authorised broad dissemination.
Selective disclosure is prohibited: no sharing of draft earnings ranges, undisclosed port concession awards, or murabaha financing term sheets with analysts, vendors, or employees beyond a documented need‑to‑know before the Tadawul announcement.
Blackout windows apply for insiders and service providers with access, from seven days before quarter‑end until two trading days after publication of financial results on the Saudi Exchange, and during any ongoing corporate action preparation.
Issuer‑relative materiality will be determined by the Disclosure Committee and recorded by Ms. Huda Al‑Sahli; cafeteria hours or facility repainting are not material, but throughput variances beyond disclosed guidance may be.
For clarity, the “Board approves interim dividends” notice dated 18 Rabi’ I 1447H and posted under Sahari on the Saudi Exchange is already public and is not MNPI.
Security controls: Processor will keep data in KSA‑hosted zones (Najd Cloud RUH‑01), apply encryption at rest and multi‑factor authentication, and maintain insider lists and access logs for not less than five years in line with CMA recordkeeping expectations.
Retention: shareholder PII is retained while legally required for registry obligations; vendor due diligence files are deleted within 90 days after contract expiry unless a CMA or judicial hold is notified.
Data subject rights: access, correction, deletion, and restriction requests will be acknowledged within five working days and resolved within PDPL timelines; identification will rely on National ID/Iqama verification without storing copies beyond verification artifacts.
Any press release that merely restates items already on the Tadawul portal does not change the public‑status timing, and operational memos about office parking are out of scope for MNPI classification.
Queries on this Addendum may be sent to Mr. Faisal Al‑Mutlaq, DPO, at privacy@najddata.example.sa, and complaints may be escalated to iaudit@sahari.example.sa.
